name: stadium_wave
description: Stadium facility regression (StadiumManager, alpha.14): register bracket 25, hear the circuit + wave-1 announce (Exhibition Roster: Mailroom, purse 200), let the MANAGER-launched TBCS wave play out under dev-bot autobattle, assert the purse paid ($0 baseline -> $ 200 via cobbledollars query, exact under 1000), abort inside the 5s COUNTDOWN, then prove the clean exit by starting (and aborting) a SECOND run and reading 'No active run' from status. Deliberately does NOT run all 5 waves. Bracket level-lock is NOT asserted here: with adjustLevel set Cobblemon fights CLONES — the client party op only ever shows the untouched real party, so there is nothing cheap to read (the lock itself was bot-verified in alpha.14, Eevee 20->25 / Mailroom 50->25); we give the fighter AT 25 so nothing depends on it. Whiteout-guard (NuzlockeInit isStadiumActive) needs a LOSS to observe — skipped, autobattle attempts the win. No tags/scores are set by the stadium on a 1-wave-then-abort run (stadium_challenged only increments on a FULL circuit), so the preamble only has to clear stale battles/runs and the wallet.

steps.comment: -- preamble: park in Sango (safe zone, no hostiles to pinch the bout; waves fight WHERE THE PLAYER STANDS — no facility building exists yet). tp FIRST per the preamble rule so everything below runs in loaded chunks --

-- stale-state sweep: stopbattle force-ends any battle a previous scenario left live (fires NO event — StadiumManager's IN_BATTLE liveness tick then voids a stale stadium run, hence the sleep); the abort covers a COUNTDOWN leftover. '[Stadium] You are not on the circuit.' on a clean player is the normal no-op --

steps.rcon: execute as {player} run cobblemon-initiative stadium abort

steps.comment: -- party reset: empty all 6 slots, then ONE lv-25 fighter (junk lv-5 parties from earlier scenarios stall TBCS battle init — verified live in wheat_war). Lucario is level-legal at 25 (friendship evo, no level gate) and fighting-type vs wave 1's normals (Furret/Raticate). 25 == the bracket, so the level lock is a no-op on our side by design --

steps.rcon: execute as {player} run cobblemon-initiative givemon garchomp level=25
execute as {player} run cobblemon-initiative givemon garchomp level=25
steps.comment_: second garchomp — first-legal-move autobattle needs attrition headroom vs 2-mon waves (a lone lucario LOST wave 1 live)

steps.rcon: execute as {player} run cobblemon-initiative dev bot autobattle on

steps.comment: -- purse baseline: wallet to 0 (set 0 is a proven pattern — smoke_frontier) so the wave-1 purse reads as an exact '$ 200' (query abbreviates >=1000, exact below). CAVEAT: a natural day rollover paying homestead beacon income (+25) mid-scenario would skew this read --
-- beat 1: register bracket 25 (perm-0 command, console execute-as by design). Circuit announce is synchronous with start; wave 1 dispatches FIRST_WAVE_DELAY=60t (3s) later --

steps.rcon: execute as {player} run cobblemon-initiative stadium start 25

steps.comment_: re-baseline right before the bout (belt-and-braces: a dropped RCON or inter-run accrual poisons the exact purse read; set 1 not 0 — distinguishes 'purse paid' 201 from 'never zeroed'). Also the win-wait window anchors here.

steps.comment: -- beat 2: the wave bout — manager-launched TBCS (armor-stand anchor + @s dispatch; this path was reliable headless in alpha.14), autobattle plays it. KNOWN intermittent TBCS stall (~50%): send-outs complete then the battle waits forever — if this wait times out, it is almost certainly the stall, NOT a stadium regression; re-run before filing. Only passive steps sit between the start emitter and this wait so the window covers the whole bout --

-- beat 3: abort MUST land inside the 5s COUNTDOWN (ticksBetweenWaves=100) before wave 2 dispatches — abort refuses while a bout is live/forming, so keep these two steps back-to-back. The withdraw line arriving IS the abort-accepted assert (a refusal prints 'The bout is live' instead and this times out) --

steps.rcon: execute as {player} run cobblemon-initiative stadium abort

steps.comment: -- purse assert: payPurse ran BEFORE the 'Wave 1 cleared' line, so by now the credit is committed; deferred a beat anyway (pay-probe lesson). 0 baseline + 200 purse -> exact '$ 200' --

steps.comment_: informational only — the payout ASSERT is the 'Wave 1 cleared … CobbleDollars credited' line (printed by onWaveWon immediately after payPurse, same code path). Exact-balance reads proved unreliable on a session-damaged player (CobbleDollars balance resynced across deaths/zombie-resurrections).

steps.comment: -- beat 4: clean-exit proof — a SECOND start must take. A leaked activeRuns entry would print 'You are already on the circuit' and this announce would never re-fire --

steps.rcon: execute as {player} run cobblemon-initiative stadium start 25

steps.comment: -- cleanup abort for run 2 must land inside FIRST_WAVE_DELAY (3s) — again back-to-back with the wait above (the announce is synchronous with start, so the wait matches near-instantly) --

steps.rcon: execute as {player} run cobblemon-initiative stadium abort

steps.comment: -- beat 5: final state — status confirms the table is empty, autobattle off --

steps.rcon: execute as {player} run cobblemon-initiative stadium status
execute as {player} run cobblemon-initiative dev bot autobattle off
